title: Anisonema reticulatum
intro: Anisonema reticulatum is a species of flowering plant in the family Annonaceae, commonly known as the Anisonema plant. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in the treatment of various ailments such as wounds, infections, and oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Anisonema reticulatum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. taxonomy: Anisonema reticulatum is classified as a species of fungus in the genus Anisonema, which belongs to the family Laboulbeniaceae. This family is part of the order Laboulbeniales and the class Laboulbeniomycetes. The class Laboulbeniomycetes is further grouped within the division Ascomycota. The division Ascomycota is a major group of fungi that includes over 64,000 known species. Anisonema reticulatum's classification helps identify its unique characteristics and relationships with other organisms in the fungal kingdom.
